data.description:
# Derive and enforce plan-material-pin health

## Goal
Use signed pins as the single related-material validity check across every attestation lifecycle consumer, while preserving safe rollout for existing projects.

## Implementation
- Add verify.enforce_plan_material_pins with default false and strict enabled-mode validation.
- Compare each signed pin with the related ticket's current narrow material. Return structured derived health that distinguishes current, drifted, missing-target, malformed, legacy-unpinned, and enforcement-disabled states.
- When enabled, drift, malformed pins, or a deleted/missing pinned target invalidate the attestation. Archived targets remain readable. When disabled, report the same observations as advisory while valid remains true.
- Treat a wholly unpinned legacy attestation as valid in either mode. Do not rewrite pins when configuration changes.
- Route claim, idempotent review reuse, progressive drift refresh, re-sign, audit/health computation, and the close-profile consumer through one validity implementation.
- Keep optional unreadable configuration fail-open with a warning, matching existing gate posture; once enforcement is successfully enabled, validation fails closed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pin-aware attestations become invalid on actual related material drift in every named lifecycle consumer when enforcement is enabled.
- [ ] Disabled enforcement reports advisory drift without changing valid, and legacy unpinned attestations remain valid before and after toggles.
- [ ] Missing/deleted, archived, malformed, duplicate, and re-enable-with-existing-pins scenarios have deterministic tests and no persisted warning events.

## Verification
- Run focused attestation invalidation, reuse, drift-refresh, re-sign, and config tests.
- Run make check and the non-integration test suite.
data.title: Derive plan-material-pin health and enforce optional drift invalidation
